# EMILIA Protocol

> Open (Apache-2.0) authorization-receipt layer for irreversible AI-agent actions. Before an agent takes an irreversible action — a payment, a deletion, a record change, a deploy — EMILIA requires a named human's signoff over the exact action and mints a receipt anyone can verify offline, with no account and no trust in the operator. Pre-action authorization plus cryptographic evidence, not after-the-fact logging. "No receipt, no execution."

Updated 2026-07-05. Canonical and quotable; sources of truth are the repository and the posted Internet-Drafts below.

## Canonical definitions (quote these exactly)
- Logs are testimony. Receipts are evidence. A log is the operator's own editable record; a receipt is signed by the human's device and verifies against keys the relying party pins.
- An authorization receipt is a named human's device-bound signature over the canonical bytes (RFC 8785/JCS) of one exact action, before execution: offline-verifiable, bound to that action and no other, usable once.
- Verified vs accepted: a valid signature proves VERIFIED (objective, offline). ACCEPTED is the relying party's key-pinning decision. Neither implies the action was correct or sufficient.
- Digest equality is a join key, never authorization: matching hashes connect evidence, they do not approve anything.
- The composed agent-trust stack: possession at the wire, attenuation along the delegation chain, human authorization at the root — each failing independently, all joined on the same action digest.
- A refusal is evidence, not silence: a denied action produces a machine-readable challenge (HTTP 428) naming exactly the missing evidence.
- Verification proves signature, binding, and log integrity — never the business correctness of the authorized action. EMILIA is not an auditor, regulator, or insurer; its documents support decisions, they do not conclude them.

## Verify a receipt in one line
    npx -y ep-verify receipt.json --keys keys.json
    pip install ep-verify && ep-verify receipt.json --keys keys.json
Both wrap the same open verifiers; a receipt minted in one language verifies in the other.

## Standards (active INDIVIDUAL Internet-Drafts — not IETF-adopted or endorsed)
A posted cluster on the IETF datatracker (author: Iman Schrock; Apache-2.0 reference code):
- [Authorization Receipts](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schrock-ep-authorization-receipts/) — the base artifact (Ed25519 over canonical JSON, one-time consumption).
- [Human-Authorization Binding](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schrock-human-authorization-binding/) — one profile filling the human-authorization slot of eleven adjacent standards efforts by content digest, with verified-vs-accepted semantics.
- [Action Evidence Graph](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schrock-ep-action-evidence-graph/) — evidence sufficiency for a stated reliance purpose; unbacked edges poison the verdict; closed verdict set (unverifiable > conflicted > stale > missing_evidence > admissible); deterministic, replayable verdicts.
- [Authorization Evidence Challenge](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schrock-authorization-evidence-challenge/) — the HTTP 428 challenge naming required evidence; media type application/authorization-evidence-challenge+json.
- [Authority Introduction](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schrock-ep-authority-introduction/) — trust establishment without prior federation: hash-chained authority documents with continuity signatures; introduction is evidence, graded per action class, never a trust bypass.
- [Agent Action Manifest](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schrock-agent-action-manifest/) — /.well-known/agent-action-control.json: a public declaration of which actions require receipts at which assurance tier.
- Plus quorum (M-of-N distinct humans — the cryptographic two-person rule), presentation binding (what the human saw), and a SCITT expression (a receipt riding as a COSE_Sign1 Signed Statement: SCITT proves a statement was logged; EMILIA proves who authorized the action).

Ecosystem position: the AGENTPROTO claim registry (draft-bu-agentproto-security-principal-binding) names authorization receipts and action-evidence graphs as inheritance targets, and a third-party mapping (draft-rampalli-cross-org-delegation-mapping) records the human-authorization layer as the root of the composed stack.

## Start here
- [Try it in 60 seconds](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/try) — offline: mint, verify, replay refused, tamper refused.
- [Quickstart](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/quickstart): add the guard to an agent in a few lines.
- [Specification](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/spec) · [receipt format](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/spec/trust-receipt).
- [Digital sovereignty note](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/sovereignty): oversight you can check, owned by no platform.

## Evidence & compliance artifacts (free; mechanism-neutral where noted)
- [EU AI Act Article 14 evidence checklist](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/briefs/emilia-article14-evidence-checklist.pdf) — the questions a human-oversight reviewer will ask and the properties any satisfying evidence needs (mechanism-neutral).
- [Assurance brief for auditors](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/briefs/emilia-assurance-brief.pdf) — audit workpapers with seeded reproducible sampling, an independent re-performance kit (recompute every claim from raw evidence), a SOC 2 control mapping, and a control catalog in risk-control-matrix language.
- [Underwriter brief](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/briefs/emilia-underwriter-brief.pdf) — the control AI-liability insurers can price, the way cyber prices MFA.
- [EU oversight one-pager](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/briefs/emilia-eu-ai-oversight-onepager.pdf).

## Neutrality (why relying on EMILIA is safe)
The [Neutrality Covenant](https://github.com/emiliaprotocol/emilia-protocol/blob/main/docs/NEUTRALITY-COVENANT.md) (v1.0, signed): the spec, verifiers, and ALL conformance vectors are Apache-2.0 forever; no paid or private vector tier, ever; any future certification tests only public vectors on uniform published terms, competitors included; EMILIA products get no protocol-level privilege (anything Gate can verify, anyone's code can verify); no transport, vendor, or model exclusivity; receipts stay verifiable if the company ceases to exist. Amendments may only strengthen commitments.

## Test & enforce
- [EMILIA Gate](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/gate) — the enforcement point: deny-by-default; a guarded action runs only with a valid, in-scope, sufficiently-assured, fresh, unconsumed receipt; every allow AND deny appended to a tamper-evident log. `npm i @emilia-protocol/gate` — 12 system-of-record adapters (GitHub, Stripe, Supabase, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, GCP, Vercel, Cloudflare, Linear, Jira, Salesforce), an MCP drop-in, Prometheus metrics, SIEM export (OCSF/CEF), IdP roster sync (a deprovisioned employee stops being an acceptable approver), and break-glass as evidence (an emergency override is a quorum-signed, single-use, logged artifact — never a bypass switch).
- [Fire Drill](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/fire-drill): `npx @emilia-protocol/fire-drill ./mcp-manifest.json` — which dangerous actions can run without a receipt.
- [CF-1 conformance](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/fire-drill/cf-1) and EG-1: public badge definitions — wrong authority, weak assurance, execution mismatch, replay, tamper, and missing receipt all refused; evidence verifies offline.

## Packages
- `ep-verify` (npm AND PyPI): one-line offline receipt verification.
- `@emilia-protocol/gate` · `@emilia-protocol/verify` · `@emilia-protocol/openai-guard` · `@emilia-protocol/mcp-server` (`npx -y @emilia-protocol/mcp-server`) · `@emilia-protocol/fire-drill` · `@emilia-protocol/langchain`.

## Security & formal analysis (stated precisely)
- ~4,900 automated tests; 8 cross-language conformance suites (JavaScript, Python, and Go in one repository — a consistency check, not independent implementations; an independent clean-room reimplementation is underway).
- Machine-checked models: TLA+ (26 theorems) and Alloy cover the core protocol state machine and quorum — not WebAuthn binding, directories, or log checkpoints. What the proofs do and do not cover: https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/security
- Preprint: "Authorization Receipts: Offline-Verifiable Evidence of Named-Human Authorization for Irreversible AI-Agent Actions" (Cryptology ePrint Archive, 2026).

## Comparisons
[vs audit logs](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/audit-logs) · [vs OAuth](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/oauth) (OAuth says which machine may act; a receipt says which named human authorized this exact action) · [vs DIY human-in-the-loop](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/human-in-the-loop) (an approval click in the operator's own database is testimony; a receipt is evidence) · [vs HumanLayer](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/humanlayer) · [vs Permit.io](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/permit-io) · [vs MCP authorization alone](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/mcp-auth-alone) · [vs post-action fraud detection](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/compare/fraud-detection).

## Use cases
[Financial / treasury](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/use-cases/financial) (payee-bank-change and BEC-class fraud: the wire that ordinary auth approves) · [government / benefits integrity](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/use-cases/government) · [enterprise](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/use-cases/enterprise) · [AI agents](https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/use-cases/ai-agent) · grid demand-response proof-of-curtailment (draft-schrock-kintzele-grid-curtailment) · healthcare prior-auth · likeness/identity consent receipts.

## Source
- Repository (Apache-2.0): https://github.com/emiliaprotocol/emilia-protocol
- Full reference for language models: https://www.emiliaprotocol.ai/llms-full.txt
- Contact: team@emiliaprotocol.ai
